config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but it should not automatically outrank individual rights, because that opens the door to abuse by governments and majorities. In my view, a healthy society protects basic rights first, then asks people to contribute to the common good within those limits. From my perspective, the best balance is strong social responsibility without sacrificing personal freedom and dignity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami justru menunjukkan bahwa ketika mayoritas atau pemerintah diberi terlalu banyak kuasa atas individu, hasilnya sering berupa penyalahgunaan, diskriminasi, dan pembatasan kebebasan yang sulit dibalik. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan perlindungan dari tindakan sewenang-wenang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengorbankan individu demi “kebaikan bersama,” melainkan yang menjaga hak individu sambil mendorong tanggung jawab sosial.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement because putting society first by default makes it too easy to excuse abuse, especially when governments or majorities claim they know what is best. In Indonesia, I have seen how appeals to order, harmony, or “the public interest” can be used to silence criticism and pressure people to conform, so individual rights need real protection, not just polite promises. Society does matter, but it is strongest when it respects personal dignity, freedom of expression, and due process. The common good should be pursued within limits that prevent the individual from being sacrificed too easily.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena hak individu tidak boleh dijadikan urutan kedua secara otomatis, bahkan atas nama “kepentingan masyarakat.” Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa mudahnya argumen demi kebaikan bersama dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, atau kekuasaan pemerintah yang berlebihan, dan itu justru merusak masyarakat dalam jangka panjang. Masyarakat memang punya kepentingan yang sah, tetapi kepentingan itu harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan perlindungan dari tindakan sewenang-wenang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menelan individu demi mayoritas, melainkan yang memaksa kepentingan umum berjalan tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan pribadi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as an Indonesian, I have seen how easy it is for “the public interest” or “social harmony” to become a cover for silencing criticism, pressuring minorities, or excusing abuse by authorities. Society is important, but it is only genuinely healthy when it protects basic rights like free expression, due process, and personal dignity instead of treating individuals as expendable. The common good should be pursued, but not at the cost of making individual rights conditional on what the majority or the state wants.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena hak individu tidak boleh otomatis dikalahkan oleh kepentingan masyarakat. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat terlalu banyak contoh ketika alasan demi kebaikan bersama dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, atau kekuasaan pemerintah yang berlebihan, dan hasilnya justru merusak kepercayaan publik. Kepentingan sosial memang nyata, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan perlindungan dari tindakan sewenang-wenang. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang mengorbankan individu demi mayoritas, melainkan yang memaksa kepentingan umum berjalan tanpa menghancurkan martabat dan kebebasan pribadi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
